title: Largest Rectangle in Histogram
description: Given an array of integers heights representing the histogram's bar heights where the width of each bar is 1, return the area of the largest rectangle that can be formed within the histogram.

examples.explanation: The largest rectangle can be formed with heights [5, 6] which gives an area of 5 * 2 = 10.
The largest rectangle is formed by the height 4 which results in an area of 4 * 1 = 4.
The largest rectangle can be formed with the height 9 at index 1 which gives an area of 9 * 1 = 9.

hints: Consider using a stack to keep track of the indices of the histogram bars.
Iterate through the heights and calculate areas when you encounter a shorter bar.
Remember to handle the case where the remaining bars in the stack need to be processed after the loop.
Think about how you can use the indices stored in the stack to calculate the width of the rectangle.
